description: Implement Shopee product copy engine, copy router, frontend tab, admin management, and permissions. Phase 1 (OAuth, API client, DB tables) is already complete.

userStories.title: Shopee copy engine — fetch source + upload images + build payload helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the core helper functions for the Shopee copy engine so that copy_single_item can orchestrate them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create file `app/services/shopee_copier.py`
Import shopee_api functions from `app/services/shopee_api.py` (get_item, get_item_extra, get_model_list, get_categories, get_logistics_channels, upload_image, add_item)

Implement `async _fetch_source_item(shop_id: int, item_id: int, org_id: str) -> dict` that calls get_item_base_info + get_item_extra_info + get_model_list and returns merged data dict with keys: base_info, extra_info, models

Implement `async _upload_images(dest_shop_id: int, image_urls: list[str], org_id: str) -> list[str]` that uploads each image via shopee_api.upload_image and returns list of image_id strings. Max 9 images. Retry 1x on failure per image. Log warnings for failed uploads.
Implement `async _fetch_dest_logistics(dest_shop_id: int, org_id: str) -> list[dict]` that calls get_logistics_channels and returns list of enabled logistics channels

Implement `_build_shopee_payload(source_data: dict, image_ids: list[str], logistics: list[dict], dimensions: dict | None = None) -> dict` that builds the add_item payload with: item_name (max 120 chars), description (max 3000 chars), original_price, normal_stock, category_id, image.image_id_list, weight (in kg), logistic_info, condition='NEW', pre_order, item_sku. Include dimension if provided (package_height/width/length in cm). Include attribute_list from source if present.
Add module-level logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1 already created `app/services/shopee_api.py` with all API wrappers: get_item, get_item_extra, get_model_list, get_logistics_channels, upload_image, add_item, _get_token. The shopee_api module handles HMAC signing and token management. The copier just orchestrates calls. Use the existing patterns from `app/services/item_copier.py` (the ML copy engine) for reference — it has similar _build_item_payload and fetch functions. Shopee returns base_info items inside response.item_list[0]. Shopee image URLs are in item.image.image_url_list. Weight is in kg (float). The description comes from get_item_extra_info response. Models/variations come from get_model_list response.

userStories.title: Shopee copy engine — copy_single_item + copy_items + logging
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the main copy orchestration functions so that the router can call them to copy products.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`: implement `async copy_single_item(source_shop_id: int, dest_shop_id: int, item_id: int, org_id: str, user_id: str = None, dimensions: dict | None = None) -> dict` that: (1) calls _fetch_source_item, (2) calls _upload_images with source image URLs, (3) calls _fetch_dest_logistics, (4) calls _build_shopee_payload, (5) calls shopee_api.add_item, (6) returns result dict with status/dest_item_id/error/sku
Implement retry logic: up to 3 attempts. Attempt 2 removes brand and non-essential attributes. Attempt 3 uses minimal payload (name, description, price, stock, category, images, weight, logistics).
On ShopeeApiError: log full error to api_debug_logs table (same pattern as ML copier: source_item_id, dest_seller, attempt_number, error_message, request_payload, response_body)

Implement `async copy_items(source_slug: str, dest_slugs: list[str], item_ids: list[int], user_id: str, org_id: str) -> dict` that: resolves shop_ids from slugs via shopee_sellers table, calls copy_single_item for each item×dest combination, logs each result to shopee_copy_logs table, returns {total, success, errors, needs_dimensions, results}

Implement `async copy_with_dimensions(source_slug: str, dest_slugs: list[str], item_id: int, dimensions: dict, user_id: str, org_id: str) -> dict` that calls copy_single_item with dimensions parameter
Add 0.7s sleep between sequential copy calls (rate limit pacing)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 helpers. The shopee_sellers table has columns: slug, shop_id, org_id. To resolve slug→shop_id: db.table('shopee_sellers').select('shop_id').eq('slug', slug).eq('org_id', org_id).single().execute(). For api_debug_logs: use same table as ML (columns: source_item_id, dest_seller, attempt_number, error_message, request_payload, response_body, resolved, created_at). For shopee_copy_logs: columns are user_id, org_id, source_seller, dest_sellers, source_item_id, status, dest_item_ids (jsonb), error_details (jsonb). Use get_db() from app.db.supabase. ShopeeApiError is imported from app.services.shopee_api. If error message contains 'dimension' or 'weight', set status to 'needs_dimensions'.

userStories.title: Shopee copy router — all endpoints
userStories.description: As a user, I want API endpoints to copy Shopee products, preview items, view logs, and resolve sellers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create file `app/routers/shopee_copy.py` with APIRouter(prefix='/api/shopee/copy', tags=['shopee-copy'])

POST `/` endpoint: accepts ShopeeCopyRequest(source: str, destinations: list[str], item_ids: list[str]). Validates permissions via user_permissions table (can_copy_from for source, can_copy_to for each dest — admin bypasses). Checks trial limit using same _check_trial_limit pattern from copy.py. Normalizes item_ids to int. Calls shopee_copier.copy_items(). Increments trial_copies_used for successful copies. Returns CopyResponse dict.

POST `/with-dimensions` endpoint: accepts ShopeeCopyWithDimensionsRequest(source: str, destinations: list[str], item_id: str, dimensions: {height: int, width: int, length: int, weight: int}). Calls shopee_copier.copy_with_dimensions(). Weight is received in grams, converted to kg (÷1000) before passing to copier.

GET `/preview/{item_id}` endpoint: auto-detects shop by trying each active shopee_seller in org. Returns: item_name, original_price, stock, category_id, status, image_url (first image), image_count, model_count, has_description, weight, shop_slug.

GET `/logs` endpoint: queries shopee_copy_logs filtered by org_id. Admins see all, operators see only their own (user_id filter). Supports ?limit=50&offset=0&status= params. Returns list of log dicts.

POST `/resolve-sellers` endpoint: accepts {item_ids: list[str]}. For each active shopee_seller with can_copy_from permission, tries get_item_base_info. Returns {results: [{item_id, shop_slug}], errors: [{item_id, error}]}. Uses asyncio.Semaphore(5) for parallel checks.
Register router in `app/main.py`: add `from app.routers import ... shopee_copy` and `app.include_router(shopee_copy.router)`
All endpoints use Depends(require_active_org) except logs which uses Depends(require_user)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Mirror the pattern from `app/routers/copy.py` which has the same endpoint structure for ML. The _check_trial_limit function is in copy.py — either import it or copy the pattern (query orgs table for payment_active, trial_copies_used, trial_copies_limit). _increment_trial_copies is also in copy.py: db.table('orgs').update({'trial_copies_used': used + count}).eq('id', org_id).execute(). For permissions: query user_permissions table with .eq('user_id', user_id).eq('seller_slug', slug) and check can_copy_from/can_copy_to. Admin role (user['role'] == 'admin') bypasses permission checks. For resolve-sellers: the shopee_api.get_item function returns response with item_list — if item_list is not empty, that shop has the item. Use try/except ShopeeApiError to handle items not found.

userStories.title: Admin permissions — include Shopee shops
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the permissions endpoints to list Shopee shops alongside ML sellers so I can manage operator access to both platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/routers/admin_users.py` GET `/users/{user_id}/permissions` endpoint: query both copy_sellers (ML) AND shopee_sellers tables for the org. Merge results, adding 'platform': 'ml' for ML sellers and 'platform': 'shopee' for Shopee shops. Each entry has: seller_slug, seller_name, platform, can_copy_from, can_copy_to.
Shopee shops use their slug from shopee_sellers table as seller_slug in user_permissions (same table, no separate permissions table).
In PUT `/users/{user_id}/permissions` endpoint: accept permissions for both ML and Shopee slugs. Upsert into user_permissions table (same as before — the seller_slug field works for both).
The response from GET permissions includes a 'platform' field so the frontend can differentiate ML from Shopee.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In admin_users.py: the GET permissions endpoint queries user_permissions joined with copy_sellers. Add a second query to shopee_sellers for the same org. Build the merged list: for each seller in copy_sellers add platform='ml', for each in shopee_sellers add platform='shopee'. Then join with existing user_permissions to get can_copy_from/can_copy_to for each. The PUT endpoint already upserts by user_id + seller_slug — this works for Shopee slugs too since user_permissions doesn't have a platform column. The slug uniqueness across platforms is sufficient (ML slugs come from ML user nicknames, Shopee slugs from shop names — very unlikely to collide).

userStories.title: Super admin — Shopee stats per org
userStories.description: As a super_admin, I want to see Shopee seller count and copy count per org in the platform management view.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/routers/super_admin.py` GET `/orgs` endpoint: add two additional queries per org — shopee_seller_count from shopee_sellers (active, same org) and shopee_copy_count from shopee_copy_logs (same org, last 30 days)
Include 'shopee_seller_count' and 'shopee_copy_count' in each org dict in the response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In super_admin.py list_orgs: after the existing queries for user_count, seller_count, copy_count, compat_count, add: shopee_seller_count = db.table('shopee_sellers').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).eq('active', True).execute().count or 0, and shopee_copy_count = db.table('shopee_copy_logs').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).gte('created_at', thirty_days_ago).execute().count or 0. Add both to the enriched dict.

userStories.title: Frontend types + auth hook — Shopee sellers state
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TypeScript types for Shopee data and Shopee seller state in the auth hook so that frontend pages can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`: add ShopeeSeller interface (slug: string, name: string, shop_id: number, token_valid: boolean, token_expires_at: string | null, created_at: string)
Add ShopeeCopyResult interface (source_item_id: string, dest_seller: string, status: 'success'|'error'|'pending'|'needs_dimensions', dest_item_id: string|null, error: string|null, sku?: string|null)
Add ShopeeCopyResponse interface (total: number, success: number, errors: number, needs_dimensions?: number, results: ShopeeCopyResult[])

Add ShopeeCopyLog interface (id: number, user_email: string|null, source_seller: string, dest_sellers: string[], source_item_id: string, dest_item_ids: Record<string,string>, status: string, error_details: Record<string,string>|null, created_at: string)

Add ShopeeItemPreview interface (item_id: number, item_name: string, original_price: number, stock: number, category_id: number, status: string, image_url: string, image_count: number, model_count: number, has_description: boolean, weight: number, shop_slug: string)
Add shopee_seller_count and shopee_copy_count fields to OrgWithStats interface
In `frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts`: add shopeeSellers state (useState<ShopeeSeller[]>([]))
Add loadShopeeSellers callback that fetches GET /api/shopee/sellers and sets state. On 401 clear auth.
Add disconnectShopeeSeller(slug) callback that calls DELETE /api/shopee/sellers/{slug} then reloads.
Call loadShopeeSellers in the same useEffect that calls loadSellers (when token && user)
Also call loadShopeeSellers on visibility change (same pattern as ML sellers)
Clear shopeeSellers in clearAuth callback
Return shopeeSellers, loadShopeeSellers, disconnectShopeeSeller from the hook
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In api.ts: add the interfaces after the existing ML ones. ShopeeSeller mirrors Seller but with shop_id instead of ml_user_id. ShopeeCopyLog mirrors CopyLog. ShopeeItemPreview is Shopee-specific. In useAuth.ts: follow the exact same pattern as loadSellers/disconnectSeller but targeting /api/shopee/sellers endpoints. Import ShopeeSeller from api.ts. The hook currently returns {isAuthenticated, initializing, token, user, login, signup, logout, sellers, loadingSellers, loadSellers, disconnectSeller, headers}. Add shopeeSellers, loadShopeeSellers, disconnectShopeeSeller to the return.

userStories.title: ShopeeCopyPage — full copy page for Shopee
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated page to copy Shopee products between shops, with form, progress, and logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `frontend/src/pages/ShopeeCopyPage.tsx` that mirrors CopyPage.tsx structure
Props: shopeeSellers: ShopeeSeller[], headers: () => Record<string,string>, user: AuthUser|null

Form section: textarea for item IDs (accepts numbers, URLs like shopee.com.br/product/X/Y extracting Y, deduplicates), dropdown/chips for destination shops (filtered by can_copy_to permissions for operators, all for admins)
Auto-resolve: on blur/paste, POST /api/shopee/copy/resolve-sellers to detect source shop per item. Show resolved source next to each item.
Copy button: POST /api/shopee/copy with source, destinations, item_ids. Show progress/results.

Results section: show status per item×dest (success with new ID, error with message, needs_dimensions with DimensionForm inline). DimensionForm reuses existing component from components/DimensionForm.tsx. On dimension submit: POST /api/shopee/copy/with-dimensions.

Logs section: collapsible, fetches GET /api/shopee/copy/logs with pagination. Filter by status dropdown. Show: date, source, dest, item_id, status, new IDs. Poll every 5s while any result is 'pending'.
Use same Card component export from CopyPage (or define locally)
Use useToast() for notifications
Item ID normalization: accept pure numbers, URLs (extract last path segment as item_id)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Mirror the structure of CopyPage.tsx as closely as possible. CopyPage uses CopyForm and CopyProgress components — for Shopee, build the form and progress inline in ShopeeCopyPage (simpler than creating separate components, and avoids over-engineering since patterns differ). Key differences from ML CopyPage: (1) uses shopeeSellers instead of sellers, (2) API endpoints are /api/shopee/copy/* instead of /api/copy/*, (3) item IDs are numbers (not MLBxxxxxx), (4) resolve-sellers returns shop_slug instead of seller_slug. The DimensionForm component at frontend/src/components/DimensionForm.tsx exports { Dimensions } type and the component — reuse it. The Card component is exported from CopyPage.tsx. The useToast hook is at frontend/src/components/Toast.tsx. Shop chips should have an orange tint (#EE4D2D at 10% opacity) to differentiate from ML's default style.

userStories.title: App.tsx — add Shopee tab to navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a 'Shopee' tab in the main navigation so I can access the Shopee copy functionality.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/App.tsx`: add 'shopee' to the View type union: type View = 'copy' | 'shopee' | 'admin' | 'compat' | 'super'
Import ShopeeCopyPage from './pages/ShopeeCopyPage'

In visibleTabs useMemo: add 'shopee' tab after 'copy'. Visibility rule: admin OR (operator with at least one permission where can_copy_from AND one where can_copy_to). Same logic as 'copy' tab — reuse the same permission check.
In the nav JSX: add Shopee ViewTab between Cópia and Compatibilidade tabs. Label: 'Shopee'.
In the content div: render ShopeeCopyPage when activeView === 'shopee'. Pass props: shopeeSellers={auth.shopeeSellers}, headers={auth.headers}, user={auth.user}

Update the empty-state screen (sellers.length === 0): change condition to check BOTH auth.sellers.length === 0 AND auth.shopeeSellers.length === 0 (show connect screen only when NO sellers of any type are connected). If only ML sellers are missing but Shopee shops exist, don't block.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In App.tsx: the visibleTabs useMemo builds an array of View values. Add tabs.push('shopee') right after tabs.push('copy') with the same condition. The nav section renders ViewTab components for each visible tab — add one for 'shopee'. The content section has conditional rendering per activeView — add {activeView === 'shopee' && <ShopeeCopyPage .../>}. The empty-state check on line ~402 checks auth.sellers.length === 0 — update to also check auth.shopeeSellers.length === 0. The auth hook now exposes shopeeSellers from US-006.

userStories.title: Admin page — Shopee sellers section + connect button
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to see and manage connected Shopee shops in the Admin panel alongside ML sellers.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx`: add a 'Lojas Shopee' section below the existing ML sellers section
Accept new props: shopeeSellers: ShopeeSeller[], loadShopeeSellers: () => Promise<void>, disconnectShopeeSeller: (slug: string) => Promise<void>
Show list of Shopee shops with: name (with orange Shopee badge), shop_id, token status, created date
Rename inline: same pattern as ML sellers, calling PUT /api/shopee/sellers/{slug}/name
Disconnect button with confirmation: calls disconnectShopeeSeller(slug), same pattern as ML
'Autorizar loja Shopee' button: fetches GET /api/shopee/install, redirects to data.redirect_url
Shopee badge: small orange (#EE4D2D) 'Shopee' label next to shop name
Visual separator between ML and Shopee sections
Update Props interface to include Shopee-related props
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In Admin.tsx: the component currently receives Props { sellers, loadSellers, disconnectSeller, headers }. Add shopeeSellers, loadShopeeSellers, disconnectShopeeSeller to Props. The ML section uses a Card component with seller list items — duplicate the pattern for Shopee. The install flow for ML: fetch /api/ml/install → redirect. For Shopee: fetch /api/shopee/install → redirect. The rename flow for ML: PUT /api/sellers/{slug}/name. For Shopee: PUT /api/shopee/sellers/{slug}/name. Also update the Admin rendering in App.tsx to pass the new props: <Admin sellers={auth.sellers} loadSellers={auth.loadSellers} disconnectSeller={auth.disconnectSeller} headers={auth.headers} shopeeSellers={auth.shopeeSellers} loadShopeeSellers={auth.loadShopeeSellers} disconnectShopeeSeller={auth.disconnectShopeeSeller} />

userStories.title: SuperAdminPage — Shopee columns
userStories.description: As a super_admin, I want to see Shopee seller count and copy count per org in the platform management table.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`: OrgWithStats already has shopee_seller_count and shopee_copy_count from US-006
In `frontend/src/pages/SuperAdminPage.tsx`: add 'Shopee Sellers' and 'Shopee Copies' columns to the orgs table
Display shopee_seller_count and shopee_copy_count values (default to 0 if undefined)
Position columns after existing compat_count column
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In SuperAdminPage.tsx: the table renders columns for each org stat. Add two new <th> and <td> elements for shopee_seller_count and shopee_copy_count. Use org.shopee_seller_count ?? 0 and org.shopee_copy_count ?? 0 for display. Keep the same styling as existing columns.

userStories.title: Update CHANGELOG.md and CLAUDE.md with all Shopee Phase 2 changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want documentation updated to reflect all Shopee integration changes.
acceptanceCriteria: In CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section: add entries under the Shopee Integration heading for: shopee_copier.py copy engine, shopee_copy.py router (all 5 endpoints), admin permissions including Shopee shops, super admin Shopee stats, frontend ShopeeCopyPage, App.tsx Shopee tab, Admin.tsx Shopee section, SuperAdminPage Shopee columns
In CLAUDE.md: update API Routes section to include all new /api/shopee/copy/* routes (POST /, POST /with-dimensions, GET /preview/{item_id}, GET /logs, POST /resolve-sellers)
In CLAUDE.md Project Structure: confirm shopee_copier.py and shopee_copy.py are listed under services/ and routers/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CHANGELOG.md already has a Shopee Integration Phase 1 section under [Unreleased]. Add Phase 2 entries below it. CLAUDE.md already documents shopee_api.py, auth_shopee.py, and the Shopee API Patterns section. Add the copy router and copier to the structure and routes sections. Keep entries concise and in Portuguese.
